theme: datacenter rack workload placement under thermal limits
title: Rack Grid Thermal Placement: Diffusion, Hysteresis, and the Coolest-Node Trap
strategies: blind round-robin: cycle jobs across nodes in id order, start every job the instant it can legally start, ignore heat entirely (baseline)

coolest-node-now greedy: track a crude private heat proxy per node (own load only, decayed by a fixed factor, no neighbor coupling, no hysteresis state), always assign the incoming job to whichever node's proxy is currently lowest, start immediately
coolest-node-now greedy with a shorter delay tolerance (same trap, slightly different pacing) -- included as a second discriminative recipe point

physics-aware simulate-and-place: for every job, actually run the SAME diffusion+hysteresis simulation the evaluator uses (over all jobs committed so far) for every candidate (node, start-time-in-a-delay-window) pair, and commit whichever option maximizes that job's own throttle-adjusted contribution -- this lets the policy see cascading neighbor heat and hysteresis lock-in before it happens, and deliberately DELAY a job (preemptively idle a node) rather than start it into a coming hotspot
